items.headline: Cameroon reinstates vice presidency after four decades
items.body: Cameroon's parliament approved a constitutional amendment creating a vice president position, with 200 lawmakers voting in favor. The president can appoint and dismiss the vice president at will and transfer power during incapacitation.

items.headline: India's central bank holds interest rate unchanged amid oil price concerns
items.body: India's Reserve Bank kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25 percent and maintained its neutral stance. The move comes as the West Asia conflict has pushed crude oil prices higher, adding inflation concerns for import-dependent economies.

uk.headline: NHS resident doctors begin six-day strike over rejected pay offer
uk.body: Resident doctors in England started a walkout after rejecting a government offer including a 3.5 percent pay rise. The strike affects nearly half the medical workforce and will cost the health service an estimated £50 million daily.

nl.headline: Dutch government appoints envoy to finalize Tata Steel green transition plan
nl.body: Former deputy prime minister Lodewijk Asscher will lead negotiations over a customized agreement requiring up to 6.5 billion euros in investment. The talks center on shutting down the most polluting coke plants and decarbonizing the IJmuiden complex, with the state offering up to two billion euros in subsidies.

nl.headline: Finance minister to object to KLM CEO bonus amid staff pay restrictions
nl.body: Minister Eelco Heinen will challenge CEO Marjan Rintel's compensation package, which rose over 30 percent to nearly 1.6 million euros. The move highlights tension between executive pay and labor demands as KLM pursues cost-cutting measures and reorganization.
